title: ipomoea tiliifolia subsp. marquesensis
intro: Ipomoea tiliifolia subsp. marquesensis, also known as the Marquesan morning glory, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Ipomoea. This plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Ipomoea tiliifolia subsp. marquesensis include relief from pain, reduction of fever, and treatment of skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or poultices for topical application, allowing for a range of uses in traditional medicine.
